title: fridericia celastroides
intro: Fridericia celastroides, also known as Chaconia or Queen of the Night, is a flowering plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is a climbing vine that belongs to the Apocynaceae family and is known for its distinctive, fragrant flowers that bloom only at night. Traditionally, Fridericia celastroides has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of Fridericia celastroides can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and improve overall well-being.
